title: Would Evangelion work as a Slice of Life series
display_title: Would Evangelion work as a Slice of Life series
wikitext:
[]: Would Evangelion work as a Slice of Life series?             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Aug 31, 2011 7:42 pm While the character interaction is very well done, I would like to know if Eva can work without the mecah and just focus on the characters? I may had answered my own question but Evangelion is a mecha and it may not work to well without all the mecha or the mystery. Even if they rmeovie the mechas, Rei, the Angels and Instrumentality, would only be left with a bunch of characters you might not evne care about. Even so, would it really work out if they focus on the characters.

[]: Re: Would Evangelion work as a Slice of Life series?             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:09 pm GAP wrote: While the character interaction is very well done, I would like to know if Eva can work without the mecah and just focus on the characters? I may had answered my own question but Evangelion is a mecha and it may not work to well without all the mecha or the mystery. Even if they rmeovie the mechas, Rei, the Angels and Instrumentality, would only be left with a bunch of characters you might not evne care about. Even so, would it really work out if they focus on the characters.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:23 pm When it comes to mechas, I'm not so sure. I don't know how the series would develop the major maternity motif without Evas. Unless they attach the characters' mothers' souls to something like cars..which would basically be the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:42 pm I think the Rei with toast was prove enough.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:53 pm In before 'Shinji Ikari Raising Project' Manga

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:59 pm An Evangelion mystery fanfiction would be pretty cool, featuring Kaji as a main character.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 31, 2011 9:13 pm EvangelionFan wrote: In before 'Shinji Ikari Raising Project' Manga

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Aug 31, 2011 9:17 pm It really depends on what you mean by "slice of life." If the show only just focused on the characters, their interactions, and their psychology, then yes, I can see it working, providing there's a way for the characters to react to each other and finding some kind of conflict to work on (and it's not difficult; you'll be surprised on many American cartoons can do this). If by "slice by life", you mean a high school setting with vanilla characterizations, then that's...debatable.

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Thu Sep 01, 2011 6:21 am That new Campus Apocalypse manga also has some SoL traits. Pretty enjoyable if you ask me.

[]: Meh. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Sep 01, 2011 6:49 am Shame about the ending though, so many things left unresolved.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Sep 01, 2011 11:24 am @Campus Apocalypse: It felt a tad rushed/cut short at the End, even tough it was so awesome. Maybe the author was told to wrap stuff up because it wasn't popular enough, or they went out of ideas. *how* the plot was resolved was epic, tough. It had some very good scenes that, with slight adjustations for the differences in setting, might as well fit into the original show. @Topic: I would personally miss the Sci/Fi stuff, but I think it would work. Sure, you'd have to adjust things a bit (Kyoko might just have had some normal mental illness, Yui has to kick the bucket a normal way etc, The NERV ppl could simply be the staff at the school, Asuka would get some regular rape instead of mental one, instead of her EVA skills, she could obsess over her school grades/popularity, Rei can't be a clone, so you have to make up a different backstory for her - A way for her to wind up with her personality would be for her to have been neglected at some orphanage or spent a long time being treated at a hospital (both of those can screw up emotional developement if no one pays proper attention to the kids and would provide a reason for why Shinji and Rei would be unfamiliar with each other) and later taken in by Gendo... wich would even let the option to reveal that she's actually somehow related to Yui.) but i say it would technically work. I mean, most ppl watch EVA for the deep, complex Characterizations and not for the mecha battles and the character's simple everyday interactions with each other take up a very significant portion of the show. It's really a comming-of-age story with a bit of philosophic contemplations here and there,and perhaps a bit of a social commentary or romance... the mechs a just a shiny plot device to make it look interesting that could technically be replaced by another.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Sep 01, 2011 1:59 pm EvangelionFan wrote: In before 'Shinji Ikari Raising Project' Manga

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 01, 2011 2:05 pm Two words : Two Salarymen.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Sep 06, 2011 12:31 pm I was kind of expectign somehtign where even the adult characters get soem development.

I may had answered my own question but Evangelion is a mecha and it may not work to well without all the mecha or the mystery. Even if they rmeovie the mechas, Rei, the Angels and Instrumentalit
